MrLesk/Backlog.md ·GitHub ⭐ 5113 TypeScript · Task Manager, Kanban, Markdown, AI Agents, MCP, Git-Native, Open Source

What it is

Backlog.md is a zero-config task management system that lives entirely within your project's Git repository as plain Markdown files. It is specifically optimized for 'spec-driven AI development,' providing a structured way to feed tasks and implementation plans to AI agents like Claude Code or Gemini CLI via MCP.

Problems solved

  • Heavy, centralized project management tools (Jira/Trello) for small developer teams
  • Disconnected task status from the actual code (tasks live in the git repo here)
  • Lack of a standardized task format for AI coding agents to follow

How to get started

Install globally via npm or brew. Run backlog init in your repo to start. You can manage tasks via CLI commands (backlog task create/list), view a terminal Kanban board (backlog board), or launch a sleek local web UI (backlog browser).

Detailed report

1. Positioning

MrLesk/Backlog.md is positioned as a backlog.md is a zero-config task management system that lives entirely within your project's git repository as plain markdown files. it is specifically optimized for 'spec-driven ai development,' providing a structured way to feed tasks and implementation plans to ai agents like claude code or gemini cli via mcp. It aims to provide developers with a robust, TypeScript-based solution for backlog.md - a tool for managing project collaboration between humans and ai agents in a git ecosystem.

2. Pain Points & Advantages

Traditional methods in this domain often suffer from Heavy, centralized project management tools (Jira/Trello) for small developer teams. MrLesk/Backlog.md addresses this by backlog.md is a zero-config task management system that lives entirely within your project's git repository as plain markdown files. it is specifically optimized for 'spec-driven ai development,' providing a structured way to feed tasks and implementation plans to ai agents like claude code or gemini cli via mcp, offering a significant performance boost and better developer ergonomics compared to existing alternatives.

3. Technical Architecture

The project is built using TypeScript, adopting a modular and scalable design. It leverages modern industry standards to ensure compatibility across different environments, focusing on efficiency and ease of integration.

4. Core Implementation

Main logic revolves around backlog.md - a tool for managing project collaboration between humans and ai agents in a git ecosystem. The implementation utilizes TypeScript's strengths to provide backlog.md is a zero-config task management system that lives entirely within your project's git repository as plain markdown files. it is specifically optimized for 'spec-driven ai development,' providing a structured way to feed tasks and implementation plans to ai agents like claude code or gemini cli via mcp, ensuring high reliability and performance under load.

5. Code Organization

The repository is structured logically, with core functionality separated from utilities and examples. This organization facilitates easy navigation and contribution for developers looking to extend the tool's capabilities.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

To get started, install globally via npm or brew. run backlog init in your repo to start. you can manage tasks via cli commands (backlog task create/list), view a terminal kanban board (backlog board), or launch a sleek local web ui (backlog browser). This will allow you to quickly evaluate the tool and integrate it into your existing development workflow.

7. Summary

An essential, TypeScript-powered utility that streamlines backlog.md - a tool for managing project collaboration between humans and ai agents in a git ecosystem; it effectively tackles Heavy, centralized project management tools (Jira/Trello) for small developer teams while maintaining a high standard of code quality and usability.
